References

  • Abbott et al. [2016] B. P. Abbott et al. (LIGO Scientific Collaboration and Virgo Collaboration), Observation of gravitational waves from a binary black hole merger, Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 061102 (2016).
  • Abbott et al. [2023a] B. P. Abbott et al. (LIGO Scientific Collaboration, Virgo Collaboration, and KAGRA Collaboration), Population of merging compact binaries inferred using gravitational waves through gwtc-3, Phys. Rev. X 13, 011048 (2023a).
  • Schutz [1986] B. F. Schutz, Determining the Hubble constant from gravitational wave observations, Nature 323, 310 (1986).
  • Abbott et al. [2017a] B. P. Abbott, R. Abbott, T. D. Abbott, et al. (LIGO Scientific Collaboration and Virgo Collaboration), Gw170817: Observation of gravitational waves from a binary neutron star inspiral, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 161101 (2017a).
  • Goldstein et al. [2017] A. Goldstein, P. Veres, E. Burns, and et al., An ordinary short gamma-ray burst with extraordinary implications: Fermi-gbm detection of grb 170817a, Astrophys. J. 848, 14 (2017).
  • Savchenko et al. [2017] V. Savchenko, C. Ferrigno, E. Kuulkers, and et al., Integral detection of the first prompt gamma-ray signal coincident with the gravitational-wave event gw170817, ApJL 848, L15 (2017).
  • Arcavi et al. [2017] I. Arcavi, G. Hosseinzadeh, D. A. Howell, and et al., Optical emission from a kilonova following a gravitational-wave-detected neutron-star merger, Nature 551, 64 (2017), arXiv:1710.05843 .
  • Coulter et al. [2017] D. A. Coulter, R. J. Foley, C. D. Kilpatrick, and et al., Swope supernova survey 2017a (sss17a), the optical counterpart to a gravitational wave source, Science 358, 1556 (2017).
  • Cowperthwaite et al. [2017] P. S. Cowperthwaite, E. Berger, V. A. Villar, and et al., The Electromagnetic Counterpart of the Binary Neutron Star Merger LIGO/Virgo GW170817. II. UV, Optical, and Near-infrared Light Curves and Comparison to Kilonova Models, ApJL 848, L17 (2017), arXiv:1710.05840 .
  • Soares-Santos et al. [2017] M. Soares-Santos, D. E. Holz, J. Annis, and et al., The electromagnetic counterpart of the binary neutron star merger ligo/virgo gw170817. i. discovery of the optical counterpart using the dark energy camera, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, 7 (2017).
  • Chornock et al. [2017] R. Chornock, E. Berger, D. Kasen, and et al., The Electromagnetic Counterpart of the Binary Neutron Star Merger LIGO/Virgo GW170817. IV. Detection of Near-infrared Signatures of r-process Nucleosynthesis with Gemini-South, ApJL 848, L19 (2017), arXiv:1710.05454 .
  • Kasliwal et al. [2017] M. M. Kasliwal, E. Nakar, L. P. Singer, and et al., Illuminating Gravitational Waves: A Concordant Picture of Photons from a Neutron Star Merger, Science 358, 1559 (2017), arXiv:1710.05454 .
  • Nicholl et al. [2017] M. Nicholl, E. Berger, D. Kasen, and et al., The Electromagnetic Counterpart of the Binary Neutron Star Merger LIGO/Virgo GW170817. III. Optical and UV Spectra of a Blue Kilonova from Fast Polar Ejecta, ApJL 848, L18 (2017), arXiv:1710.05456 .
  • Evans et al. [2017] P. A. Evans, S. B. Cenko, J. A. Kennea, and et al., Swift and NuSTAR observations of GW170817: Detection of a blue kilonova, Science 358, 1565 (2017), arXiv:1710.05437 .
  • Pian et al. [2017] E. Pian, Avanzo, P. D, Benetti, S., and et al., Spectroscopic identification of r-process nucleosynthesis in a double neutron-star merger, Nature 551, 67–70 (2017), arXiv:1710.05858 .
  • Smartt et al. [2017] S. J. Smartt, T.-W. Chen, A. Jerkstrand, and et al., A kilonova as the electromagnetic counterpart to a gravitational-wave source, Nature 551, 75 (2017), arXiv:1710.05841 .
  • Tanvir et al. [2017] N. R. Tanvir, A. J. Levan, C. Gonzalez-Fernandez, and et al., The Emergence of a Lanthanide-Rich Kilonova Following the Merger of Two Neutron Stars, ApJL 848, L27 (2017), arXiv:1710.05455 .
  • Valenti et al. [2017] S. Valenti, D. J. Sand, S. Yang, and et al., The Discovery of the Electromagnetic Counterpart of GW170817: Kilonova AT 2017gfo/DLT17ck, ApJL 848, L24 (2017).
  • Abbott et al. [2017b] B. Abbott, R. Abbott, T. D. Abbott, and et al., A gravitational-wave standard siren measurement of the Hubble constant, Nature 551, 85 (2017b), arXiv:1710.05835 .
  • Abbott et al. [2023b] R. Abbott, T. D. Abbott, F. Acernese, and et al. (LIGO Scientific Collaboration, Virgo Collaboration, and KAGRA Collaboration), Gwtc-3: Compact binary coalescences observed by ligo and virgo during the second part of the third observing run, Phys. Rev. X 13, 041039 (2023b).
  • Ezquiaga [2021] J. Ezquiaga, Hearing gravity from the cosmos: GWTC-2 probes general relativity at cosmological scales, Phys. lett. B 822, 136665 (2021).
  • Ezquiaga and Holz [2022] J. Ezquiaga and D. Holz, Spectral Sirens: Cosmology from the Full Mass Distribution of Compact Binaries, Phys. Rev. Lett. 129, 061102 (2022).
  • Farah et al. [2025] A. Farah, T. Callister, J. Ezquiaga, and et al., No need to know: astrophysics-free gravitational-wave cosmology, ApJ 978, 153 (2025).
  • Magãna Hernandez and Ray [2024] I. Magãna Hernandez and A. Ray, Beyond Gaps and Bumps: Spectral Siren Cosmology with Non-Parametric Population Models, arXiv e-prints , arXiv:2404.02522 (2024), arXiv:2404.02522 [astro-ph.GA] .
  • Borghi et al. [2024] N. Borghi, M. Mancarella, M. Moresco, and et al., Cosmology and astrophysics with standard sirens and galaxy catalogs in view of future gravitational wave observations, ApJ 964, 191 (2024).
  • Mukherjee et al. [2021a] S. Mukherjee, B. D. Wandelt, S. M.  , B. D. Nissanke, and A. Silvestri, Accurate precision cosmology with redshift unknown gravitational wave sources, PRD 103, 043520 (2021a).
  • Mukherjee et al. [2021b] S. Mukherjee, B. D. Wandelt, and J. Silk, Testing the general theory of relativity using gravitational wave propagation from dark standard sirens, MNRAS 502, 1136–1144 (2021b).
  • Mukherjee et al. [2024] S. Mukherjee, A. Krolewski, B. D. Wandelt, and J. Silk, Cross-correlating Dark Sirens and Galaxies: Constraints on H0 from GWTC-3 of LIGO–Virgo–KAGRA, ApJ 975, 189 (2024).
  • Ghosh et al. [2025] T. Ghosh, S. More, S. Bera, and S. Bose, Bayesian framework to infer the hubble constant from the cross-correlation of individual gravitational wave events with galaxies, Phys. Rev. D 111, 063513 (2025).
  • Ferri et al. [2025] J. Ferri, I. L. Tashiro, L. R. Abramo, and et al., A robust cosmic standard ruler from the cross-correlations of galaxies and dark sirens, JCAP 008.
  • Bom and Palmese [2024] C. R. Bom and A. Palmese, Standard siren cosmology with gravitational waves from binary black hole mergers in active galactic nuclei, Phys. Rev. D 110, 083005 (2024), arXiv:2307.01330 [astro-ph.CO] .
  • Palmese et al. [2021] A. Palmese, M. Fishbach, C. J. Burke, J. Annis, and X. Liu, Do LIGO/Virgo Black Hole Mergers Produce AGN Flares? The Case of GW190521 and Prospects for Reaching a Confident Association, ApJL 914, L34 (2021), arXiv:2103.16069 [astro-ph.HE] .
  • Del Pozzo [2012] W. Del Pozzo, Inference of cosmological parameters from gravitational waves: Applications to second generation interferometers, Phys. Rev. D 86, 043011 (2012), arXiv:1108.1317 .
  • Chen et al. [2018] H.-Y. Chen, M. Fishbach, and D. E. Holz, A two per cent Hubble constant measurement from standard sirens within five years, Nature 562, 545 (2018), arXiv:1712.06531 [astro-ph.CO] .
  • Fishbach et al. [2019] M. Fishbach, R. Gray, I. Magaña Hernandez, and et al., A Standard Siren Measurement of the Hubble Constant from GW170817 without the Electromagnetic Counterpart, ApJL 871, L13 (2019), arXiv:1807.05667 [astro-ph.CO] .
  • Soares-Santos et al. [2019] M. Soares-Santos, A. Palmese, W. Hartley, and et al., First Measurement of the Hubble Constant from a Dark Standard Siren using the Dark Energy Survey Galaxies and the LIGO/Virgo Binary-Black-hole Merger GW170814, ApJL 876, L7 (2019), arXiv:1901.01540 .
  • Palmese et al. [2020] A. Palmese, J. deVicente, M. E. S. Pereira, and et al., A Statistical Standard Siren Measurement of the Hubble Constant from the LIGO/Virgo Gravitational Wave Compact Object Merger GW190814 and Dark Energy Survey Galaxies, ApJL 900, L33 (2020), arXiv:2006.14961 [astro-ph.CO] .
  • Palmese et al. [2023] A. Palmese, C. R. Bom, S. Mucesh, and W. G. Hartley, A standard siren measurement of the hubble constant using gravitational-wave events from the first three ligo/virgo observing runs and the desi legacy survey, ApJ 943, 56 (2023).
  • Ballard et al. [2023] W. Ballard, A. Palmese, I. Magaña Hernandez, and et al., A Dark Siren Measurement of the Hubble Constant with the LIGO/Virgo Gravitational Wave Event GW190412 and DESI Galaxies, Res. Notes AAS 11, 250 (2023).
  • Alfradique et al. [2024] V. Alfradique, C. R. Bom, A. Palmese, G. Teixeira, and et al., A dark siren measurement of the Hubble constant using gravitational wave events from the first three LIGO/Virgo observing runs and DELVE, MNRAS 528, 3249–3259 (2024).
  • Bom et al. [2024] C. R. Bom, V. Alfradique, A. Palmese, G. Teixeira, and et al., A dark standard siren measurement of the Hubble constant following LIGO/Virgo/KAGRA O4a and previous runs, MNRAS 535, 961–975 (2024).
  • Borhanian et al. [2020] S. Borhanian, A. Dhani, A. Gupta, and et al., Dark Sirens to Resolve the Hubble–Lemaître Tension, ApJL 905, L28 (2020).
  • Muttoni et al. [2023] N. Muttoni, D. Laghi, N. Tamanini, and et al., Dark siren cosmology with binary black holes in the era of third-generation gravitational wave detectors, PRD 108, 043543 (2023).
  • Zhu and Chen [2023] L.-G. Zhu and X. Chen, The Dark Side of Using Dark Sirens to Constrain the Hubble–Lemaître Constant, ApJ 948, 26 (2023).
  • Gair et al. [2023] J. R. Gair, A. Ghosh, R. Gray, and et al., The Hitchhiker’s Guide to the Galaxy Catalog Approach for Dark Siren Gravitational-wave Cosmology, The Astronomical Journal 166, 22 (2023), arXiv:2212.08694 [gr-qc] .
  • Gray et al. [2020] R. Gray, I. Magaña Hernandez, H. Qi, and et al., Cosmological inference using gravitational wave standard sirens: A mock data analysis, PRD 101, 122001 (2020).
  • Hanselman et al. [2025] A. G. Hanselman, A. Vijaykumar, M. Fishbach, and D. Holz, Gravitational-wave dark siren cosmology systematics from galaxy weighting, ApJ 979, 9 (2025).
  • Perna et al. [2025] G. Perna, S. Mastrogiovanni, and A. Ricciardone, Investigating the impact of galaxies’ compact binary hosting probability for gravitational-wave cosmology, A&A 698, A128 (2025).
  • Turski et al. [2023] C. Turski, M. Bilicki, G. Dálya, and et al., Impact of modelling galaxy redshift uncertainties on the gravitational-wave dark standard siren measurement of the Hubble constant, MNRAS 526, 6224–6233 (2023).
  • Lima et al. [2022] E. V. R. Lima, L. Sodré Jr., C. R. Bom, and et al., Photometric redshifts for the S-PLUS Survey: Is machine learning up to the task?, Astron. Comput. 38, 100510 (2022).
  • Teixeira et al. [2024] G. Teixeira, C. Bom, L. Santana-Silva, and et al., Photometric redshifts probability density estimation from recurrent neural networks in the decam local volume exploration survey data release 2, Astronomy and Computing 49, 100886 (2024), arXiv:2408.15243 [astro-ph.IM] .
  • Li et al. [2024] C. Li, Y. Zhang, C. Cui, and et al., A photometric redshift catalogue of galaxies from the desi legacy imaging surveys dr10, ApJ 168, 233 (2024).
  • Singer and Price [2016] L. P. Singer and L. R. Price, Rapid bayesian position reconstruction for gravitational-wave transients, Phys. Rev. D 93, 024013 (2016).
  • Gray et al. [2023] R. Gray, F. Beirnaert, C. Karathanasis, and et al., Joint cosmological and gravitational-wave population inference using dark sirens and galaxy catalogues, JCAP 108, 042002.
  • Mastrogiovanni et al. [2023] S. Mastrogiovanni, D. Laghi, R. Gray, and et al., Joint population and cosmological properties inference with gravitational waves standard sirens and galaxy surveys, Phys. Rev. D 108, 042002 (2023).
  • Hirschmann et al. [2014] M. Hirschmann, K. Dolag, A. Saro, and et al., Cosmological simulations of black hole growth: Agn luminosities and downsizing, MNRAS 442, 2304–2324 (2014).
  • Ragagnin et al. [2017] A. Ragagnin, K. Dolag, V. Biffi, and et al., A web portal for hydrodynamical, cosmological simulations, Astron. Comput. 20, 52 (2017).

  • Note [2] https://lscsoft.docs.ligo.org/ligo.skymap/https://lscsoft.docs.ligo.org/ligo.skymap/.

  • Essick and Fishbach [2024] R. Essick and M. Fishbach, Ensuring consistency between noise and detection in hierarchical bayesian inference, ApJ 962, 169 (2024).
  • Finke et al. [2021] A. Finke, S. Foffa, F. Iacovelli, and et al., Cosmology with ligo/virgo dark sirens: Hubble parameter and modified gravitational wave propagation, JCAP 08, 026.
  • Gray et al. [2022] R. Gray, C. Messenger, and J. Veitch, A pixelated approach to galaxy catalogue incompleteness: improving the dark siren measurement of the hubble constant, MNRAS 512, 1127–1140 (2022).
  • Wilk and Gnanadesikan [1968] M. B. Wilk and R. Gnanadesikan, Probability plotting methods for the analysis for the analysis of data, Biometrika 55, 1 (1968).
  • Synthetic [2025] A. Synthetic Author and B. Collaborator, A test reference with plain-text arXiv ID for Plan 18 validation, arXiv:2501.99999.
  • Synthetic Old [2006] C. Old Author, A classic reference with old-format arXiv ID, hep-ph/0601234, 10.1103/PhysRevD.99.012345.
